prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: high-fidelity portrait mobile app concept screen
Primary request: Create a viral Gen Z mobile UI mockup for WakeSaga, an AI alarm and morning motivation app. Screen 1: Tonight's Trailer Setup. The user is setting up tomorrow's personalized morning episode before bed.
Design direction: "Trailer Drop" — every morning feels like a personal movie trailer premiere, but the UI must still feel like a real usable mobile app, not a poster.
Required readable UI text: "Tomorrow's Episode", "Mission", "Obstacle", "First Action", "Finish the hard thing before noon", "Phone spiral", "Feet on floor, water, desk", "Hype Friend", "Drill Coach", "Movie Trailer", "Older Me", "Wake trailer ready by 6:45 AM", "Generate Wake Trailer", "Today", "Lock In", "Archive".
Visual language: editorial streaming-app energy, bold typography, cinematic but practical, tactile controls, strong contrast, high-end Gen Z taste, confident use of off-white, black, signal red, electric green, cobalt, and small sticker-like accents.
Composition/framing: portrait iPhone-sized screen, no device frame, large header, three stacked setup fields, narrator chips, readiness row, clear sticky CTA, minimal bottom nav. Make all text large enough to read and contained inside UI elements.
Constraints: Real app interface, readable English only, clear spacing and tap targets, no fake metrics, no random numbers except wake time, no copyrighted anime characters, no fake Japanese text, no generic neon AI gradient, no fantasy RPG HUD, no bland meditation beige palette, no watermark.

Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: high-fidelity portrait mobile app concept screen
Primary request: Create a viral Gen Z mobile UI mockup for WakeSaga. Screen 2: Alarm Armed / Episode Poster. The user has generated tomorrow's AI wake trailer and locked the alarm.
Design direction: "Trailer Drop" — a personal episode poster inside a real alarm app interface.
Required readable UI text: "NO MORE SNOOZE ARC", "6:45 AM", "Mission", "Finish the hard thing before noon", "Narrator", "Movie Trailer", "Wake Action", "Stand up + confirm", "Audio generated", "Tomorrow's Beats", "Jolt", "Wake Gate", "Morning Episode", "Edit Mission", "Lock Alarm".
Visual language: bold editorial layout, cinematic poster treatment, sharp type hierarchy, collectible daily-card feeling, confident Gen Z taste, off-white paper, black ink, signal red, electric green, cobalt accents, sticker and tape details used sparingly.
Composition/framing: portrait iPhone-sized screen, no device frame, large poster card taking top half, alarm utility controls below, two clear bottom buttons. Make it functional, with real buttons and hierarchy, not just a poster.
Constraints: readable English only, no fake metrics, no random stats, no copyrighted anime IP, no fake Japanese text, no generic neon AI gradients, no dark fantasy RPG HUD, no meditation app styling, no watermark, no overlapping text.

Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: high-fidelity portrait mobile app concept screen
Primary request: Create a viral Gen Z mobile UI mockup for WakeSaga. Screen 3: Morning Wake Gate. The alarm is ringing with a personalized AI audio jolt, and the user must complete a small wake-up action to unlock the Morning Episode.
Design direction: "Trailer Drop" — cinematic alarm utility with a social, high-energy Gen Z interface.
Required readable UI text: "6:45", "Wake Jolt Playing", "You said today starts before the phone wins.", "Stand up to unlock", "Action 1 of 1", "I'm Up", "Snooze 5", "Morning Episode unlocks after this".
Visual language: urgent but not hostile, cinematic, clean, tactile, high contrast, off-white, black, signal red, electric green, cobalt, chunky buttons, bold waveform/player design, sticker-like action badge.
Composition/framing: portrait iPhone-sized screen, no device frame, big alarm time at top, prominent waveform audio player, narrator line, central wake action module, clear primary CTA, smaller snooze control, next-step preview. Make it look like a real alarm app screen with functional UI.
Constraints: readable English only, no fake metrics or random stats, no copyrighted anime IP, no fake Japanese text, no generic neon AI gradients, no fantasy RPG HUD, no bland meditation styling, no watermark, no overlapping text.

Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: high-fidelity portrait mobile app concept screen
Primary request: Create a viral Gen Z mobile UI mockup for WakeSaga. Screen 4: Morning Episode + Share Card. The user completed the wake gate and is listening to the cinematic Morning Episode. The app also generates a collectible share card for the day.
Design direction: "Trailer Drop" — editorial streaming-app meets collectible daily artifact.
Required readable UI text: "Morning Episode", "No More Snooze Arc", "2 min setup for the day you said you wanted", "Mission", "Obstacle", "First Move", "Start First Move", "Today's Wake Card", "6:45 AM", "Mission: Hard thing before noon", "Narrator: Movie Trailer", "Save", "Share".
Visual language: shareable Gen Z product UI, cinematic but grounded, bold typography, tactile audio player, collectible card preview, off-white paper, black ink, signal red, electric green, cobalt accents, small sticker/tape details.
Composition/framing: portrait iPhone-sized screen, no device frame, top header, audio episode player with progress bar and chapter markers, primary CTA, collectible card preview below with save/share buttons. Make it a real app screen with clear spacing, readable text, and implementable components.
Constraints: readable English only, no fake metrics, no arbitrary scores, no random social proof, no copyrighted anime IP, no fake Japanese text, no generic neon AI gradients, no fantasy RPG HUD, no bland meditation app styling, no watermark, no overlapping text.
